content_en:
Muruj al-Dhahab was recognized by later scholars as one of the most valuable historical and geographical works of the classical Islamic tradition. Its breadth, the quality of its observations, and the reliability of many of its accounts on subjects where other sources are thin made it an indispensable reference for historians, geographers, and encyclopedists who came after al-Mas'udi. The great polymath al-Biruni cited him respectfully, and later historians who wrote universal histories frequently drew on Muruj al-Dhahab for its accounts of pre-Islamic civilizations and its geographical information.

Al-Mas'udi's method influenced the development of Islamic historical writing in the direction of a more synthetic and cross-cultural approach. His willingness to engage seriously with non-Islamic civilizations, his interest in the natural environment as a factor in historical change, and his emphasis on firsthand observation as an epistemological standard were not always followed by later historians, but they represented a distinct methodological position that continued to attract attention and admiration.

In the modern period, Muruj al-Dhahab has been studied intensively by historians of the medieval Islamic world, historians of pre-Islamic Arabia and Persia, and scholars interested in the history of Islamic geographical thought. Its accounts of East Africa, India, and China have been of particular interest to scholars tracing the history of Islamic trade and cultural exchange across the Indian Ocean world. The work's evidence on the Shia communities of Iraq and the theological debates of the fourth Islamic century has also attracted scholarly attention.

Al-Mas'udi's Theology — he was a Mutazili in his theological orientation, though this did not prevent his acceptance in the broader Islamic scholarly tradition — is visible in parts of Muruj al-Dhahab and in his other surviving work, Kitab at-Tanbih wal-Ishraf. This theological dimension has been explored in modern scholarship on the relationship between theology and historical thought in classical Islam.
title_id: Penerimaan Keilmuan dan Pengaruh yang Bertahan
content_id:
Muruj al-Dhahab diakui oleh ulama-ulama kemudian sebagai salah satu karya paling berharga yang dihasilkan oleh tradisi historiografi Arab-Islam. Cakupannya yang luar biasa, kualitas pengamatan al-Mas'udi, dan pendekatan analitisnya yang relatif canggih membuat karya tersebut menjadi sumber yang digunakan secara luas oleh sejarawan dan ahli geografi Muslim kemudian.

Di antara yang mengutip dan mengandalkan Muruj al-Dhahab adalah beberapa sejarawan dan ahli geografi terbesar dalam tradisi Islam. Al-Biruni, Ibn Khaldun, dan Yaqut al-Hamawi semuanya menggunakan karya al-Mas'udi, dan penerimaan mereka terhadapnya menunjukkan pengakuan atas nilainya di antara kalangan terbaik tradisi intelektual Islam. Ibn Khaldun secara eksplisit memuji metode al-Mas'udi dalam satu bagian dari Al-Muqaddimah-nya, mengidentifikasinya sebagai contoh dari jenis penyelidikan sejarah yang beliau dukung.

Penemuan Muruj al-Dhahab oleh para sarjana Eropa pada abad ke-19 menghasilkan gelombang apresiasi baru. Terjemahan ke dalam bahasa Prancis oleh Barbier de Meynard dan Pavet de Courteille (diterbitkan antara 1861 dan 1877) membuat karya tersebut dapat diakses oleh para sarjana yang tidak bisa membaca bahasa Arab, dan banyak yang terkesan dengan kualitasnya. Perbandingan dengan Herodotus dan penulis sejarah kuno lainnya menjadi umum, meskipun perbandingan ini kadang-kadang menyesatkan karena al-Mas'udi bekerja dalam tradisi intelektual yang berbeda.

Pengaruh Muruj al-Dhahab terhadap historiografi Arab-Islam sulit diukur secara tepat tetapi jelas signifikan. Model al-Mas'udi tentang sejarah yang mengintegrasikan geografi, etnografi, dan narasi peristiwa mempengaruhi penulis-penulis kemudian yang berjuang dengan cara menyajikan informasi tentang dunia yang sangat besar. Pendekatannya yang menganggap serius tradisi non-Arab dan non-Muslim sebagai subjek studi yang sah memberikan contoh bagi mereka yang mengikutinya untuk terlibat dengan dunia di luar batas-batas tradisional beasiswa Muslim.

Bagi pelajar sejarah Islam kontemporer, Muruj al-Dhahab tetap menjadi sumber utama yang tak tergantikan untuk abad ke-9 dan ke-10. Nilai utamanya adalah sebagai catatan tentang apa yang diketahui oleh sarjana Muslim abad ke-10 yang terpelajar tentang dunia — baik dunia Islam maupun di luarnya — dan bagaimana beliau mendekati penyelidikan tentang alam semesta. Ini menjadikan karya tersebut sama berharganya untuk sejarah intelektual Islam seperti untuk sejarah peristiwa yang direkamnya.
content_ar: Muruj al-Dhahab was recognized by later العلماء as one of the most valuable historical and geographical works of the الكلاسيكي Islamic التراث. Its breadth, the quality of its observations, and the reliability of many of its accounts on subjects where other sources are thin made it an indispensable reference for historians, geographers, and encyclopedists who came بعد al-Mas'udi. The great polymath al-Biruni cited him respectfully, and later historians who wrote universal histories frequently drew on Muruj al-Dhahab for its accounts of pre-Islamic civilizations and its geographical information. Al-Mas'udi's method influenced the development of Islamic historical writing in the direction of a more synthetic and cross-cultural approach. His willingness to engage seriously with non-Islamic civilizations, his interest in the natural environment as a factor in historical change, and his emphasis on firsthand observation as an epistemological standard were not always followed by later historians, but they represented a distinct methodological position that continued to attract attention and admiration. In the modern period, Muruj al-Dhahab has been studied intensively by historians of the medieval Islamic الدنيا, historians of pre-Islamic Arabia and Persia, and العلماء interested in the history of Islamic geographical thought. Its accounts of East Africa, India, and China have been of particular interest to العلماء tracing the history of Islamic trade and cultural exchange across the Indian Ocean الدنيا. The work's evidence on the Shia communities of Iraq and the theological debates of the رابعاً Islamic century has أيضاً attracted العلمي attention. Al-Mas'udi's Theology — he was a Mutazili in his theological orientation, though this did not prevent his acceptance in the broader Islamic العلمي التراث — is visible in parts of Muruj al-Dhahab and in his other surviving work, Kitab at-Tanbih wal-Ishraf. This theological dimension has been explored in modern scholarship on the relationship between theology and historical thought in الكلاسيكي الإسلام.
